summary: Diff introduces backend helper functions to treat the configured creator principal as admin-or-creator, and adjusts frontend AdminGate/useAdmin/AppHeader gating to rely on definitive admin checks and authentication state. However, due to backend truncation, the diff does not provide evidence that all admin-only backend methods and the isCallerAdmin/checkPremiumStatus behaviors were actually updated to use the new creator logic.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update backend admin authorization so the configured creator principal is always treated as admin-equivalent for all admin-only backend methods, even if not in the AccessControl admin set.
missing_requirements.reason: backend/main.mo shows new helper functions (isCallerAdminOrCreator / ensureAdminOrCreator), but the truncated diff does not show admin-only methods being updated to call ensureAdminOrCreator (or otherwise incorporate creator authorization) across the listed admin-only areas (Stripe config, referral management, adminPremiumDiagnosis, manual premium grant/revoke, prediction/data management).
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated) — shows helper functions but not their application to admin-only methods

missing_requirements.requirement: Ensure actor.isCallerAdmin() returns true for the configured creator principal; and checkPremiumStatus continues to return 'creator' for that principal and never 'none'.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff summary does not include any visible changes to the implementation of the backend isCallerAdmin() method or checkPremiumStatus. Frontend still calls actor.isCallerAdmin(), but there is no evidence in the shown backend snippet that isCallerAdmin() was modified to treat the creator principal as admin, nor that checkPremiumStatus behavior was verified/maintained.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated) — contains creatorPrincipal and helper functions but no visible isCallerAdmin() or checkPremiumStatus implementation changes
